name: Pharia-1-LLM-7B (control)
archDisplay: Dense decoder-only transformer, 27 layers, 4-head grouped-query attention
note: Open, EU-compliance-focused 7B trained on lawfully sourced data; Aleph Alpha's strategic successor to Luminous.

results.notes: 25-shot, acc_norm = 0.546 on the HF model card's 'General Knowledge Benchmarks' table.
6-shot, prob_mass = 0.547 on the HF model card.
5-shot, acc = 0.651 on the HF model card.
EU21-MMLU, Thellmann et al. 2024, Table 13 ('Pharia-1-LLM-7B-control'). Matches this catalog entry's linked Aleph-Alpha/Pharia-1-LLM-7B-control exactly.

AlpacaEval 2.0, English test set, length-controlled win rate, from the official HF model card's AlpacaEval 2.0 table (not currently tracked for this model). Baseline is llama-3.1-8b-instruct (29.18% raw win rate). The same table also reports vs. Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3: 28.38% LC / 26.92% WR, plus German/French/Spanish translated variants scoring higher (~50-55% LC). Non-standard baselines (not the canonical gpt4-turbo AlpacaEval baseline), but genuine AlpacaEval 2.0 methodology as run and published by Aleph Alpha themselves.

verificationNotes: Existence and Aug 26, 2024 release confirmed via aleph-alpha.com blog post and corroborating coverage (VentureBeat, Maginative, dataphoenix, and Aleph Alpha's own X/Twitter announcement). 7.04B params (7,041,544,704 exact) and Open Aleph License (non-commercial research/educational use) confirmed by fetching the HF model card directly. All three benchmark scores (MMLU 48.4, HellaSwag 64.6, GSM8K 1.4) confirmed to literally appear on huggingface.co/Aleph-Alpha/Pharia-1-LLM-7B-control - exact match, no changes needed.
